participants.name: Garrett DiGiorgio
participants.notes: NFL Combine invitee. Per his own quotes, only did shuttle drills at pro day ('I only had two tests in terms of the shuttles. Everything else I did at the combine.'). Ran 5.36 40 at combine. Listed 6-7, 320. Five-year UCLA player, started 49 games across RT/LT/RG. Specific pro day shuttle times not publicly reported.

participants.name: Gary Smith III
participants.notes: NFL Combine invitee. Recorded 28-inch vertical at combine. Duke transfer who missed 2024 season with injury. Described as 'squatty, space-eating nose tackle.' Did change-of-direction drills and positional drills at pro day. Specific pro day numbers not publicly reported.

participants.name: Anthony Frias II
participants.notes: Standout performer. 30 bench reps would have ranked 2nd among all combine invitees and 1st among RBs. Self-reported broad jump of 10 feet (120 inches). Said he was 'still waiting to hear on my 40' after the event. Height/weight from ESPN (5-10, 225). Transfer from Kansas State.

additional_notes: UCLA held its 2026 Pro Day on Wednesday, March 11, 2026, at Wasserman Football Center in Los Angeles. 18 draft-eligible Bruins participated in front of scouts from all 32 NFL teams. Notable attendees included Washington Commanders personnel (Dan Quinn, Adam Peters, Lance Newmark) and New England Patriots GM Eliot Wolf. Activities began indoors with measurements (heights, weights, wingspans), bench press, and vertical jump, then moved outside to Spaulding Field for broad jump (on turf), 40-yard dash (on grass), shuttle run (on turf), and L-drill/3-cone (on turf), followed by positional drills. Garrett DiGiorgio and Gary Smith III were the only UCLA Combine invitees; DiGiorgio indicated he only re-tested shuttle drills at pro day. Anthony Frias II was the standout, with 30 bench reps and a self-reported 10-foot broad jump. No UCLA players were ranked in the top 257 of the NFL Mock Draft Database's consensus big board; DiGiorgio was the highest at No. 301. CRITICAL DATA GAP: Full numerical results (specific 40 times, measurements, vertical/broad for most players, shuttle/cone times) were not published in any publicly accessible source. The most detailed results appear to be behind paywalls at 247Sports (Bruin247) and On3 (Bruin Report Online). The 40-yard dash was run on grass, which may affect times compared to electronic indoor timing. Timing method for the 40 is unknown (likely hand-timed given outdoor grass setting). UCLA went 5-7 in the 2025 season under interim coaching after DeShaun Foster was dismissed; new head coach Bob Chesney was present at the pro day.
